summary: The PoC bundle demonstrates that the Gogs HTTP server has no ReadTimeout, ReadHeaderTimeout, or WriteTimeout, enabling Slowloris-style denial of service. The reproduction builds Gogs, starts it, and runs a Slowloris attack that holds connections open to exhaust server resources.
expected_security_impact: Running the Slowloris attack should cause the Gogs server to become unresponsive to legitimate requests because each slow connection holds a goroutine and file descriptor without any timeout. The server will be unable to accept new connections once resources are exhausted.
actual_observed_result: The reproduction was not executed because the Docker base image could not be pulled in the available environment.
verification_method: The verify.sh script (1) confirms no timeout configuration exists in cmd/gogs/web.go via grep, (2) starts Gogs, (3) runs slowloris.py to hold connections open, (4) attempts a healthcheck request to verify the server became unresponsive.
limitations_or_blockers: Docker base image pull timed out in the execution environment, preventing the PoC from being built and run.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh copies the local target repository and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
